text.title: Micro-MVP Anti-Patterns
text.summary: Failure modes that produce invalid learning or waste resources, with root causes.

antipattern.description: Validation theatre: a beautifully designed landing page with no distribution plan. The page converts at 0.1% from cold ads but the team declares "validated."
antipattern.reason: The traffic source determines the signal quality more than the asset does. The same page hits 10% on Hacker News Show HN and 0.5% on cold ads. Require an explicit traffic source in the pre-registration before any asset is built.

antipattern.description: Compound experiments: testing two assumptions simultaneously (e.g. demand AND price sensitivity on the same landing page).
antipattern.reason: When the hypothesis is wrong, you cannot identify which assumption failed. Single-assumption experiments produce actionable decisions; compound experiments produce ambiguous data that justifies continuing.

antipattern.description: Post-hoc threshold setting: deciding whether the experiment "succeeded" after seeing the conversion data.
antipattern.reason: Survivorship bias makes any result look like it was close to success. Pre-registration removes the ability to move the goalposts after seeing the outcome.

antipattern.description: Inflated asset bundle: building case studies, customer logos, and testimonials for a one-week experiment.
antipattern.reason: A micro-MVP needs a headline, sub-headline, three value-prop bullets, and a CTA. Additional content delays launch and adds signal noise. Cap copy explicitly.

antipattern.description: Fake-door experiment with no ethical unwind plan. Users click "Get Early Access" and receive no follow-up.
antipattern.reason: Trust violation. Transparency is required: inform users the feature does not exist yet and offer them something real (a waitlist, a refund, a free trial). Plan this before launch.

antipattern.description: Declaring "inconclusive, extend" after every failed experiment. Maximum one 7-day extension; then make a decision on available data.
antipattern.reason: Indefinite extensions are equivalent to having no success criterion. The purpose of the experiment is to produce a decision under uncertainty, not to wait for certainty.
